name: A Bag of Bean Tricks
recipeInstructions:
1. Inspect the beans for any damage or foreign material, then wash them in cold water.
2. Soak the beans using either the hot-soak method (preferred) or the quick-soak method:
a. Add 10 cups of hot water to the beans and let soak for at least 1 hour (quick-soak), or preferably four hours or more.
b. Discard the soak water before proceeding.
3. Rinse the soaked beans, then drain them well.
4. In a large pot, combine the drained beans with 6 cups of hot water, 1 to 2 tablespoons of shortening, and 2 teaspoons of salt (up to 2 teaspoons per pound of beans).
5. Bring the mixture to a boil gently with the lid slightly tilted until the beans reach the desired tenderness.
- For savory flavor, use 2 teaspoons onion salt and 1/4 teaspoon garlic salt instead of plain salt. Add 1 tablespoon of chicken stock base or 3 to 4 bouillon cubes, and 1/4 teaspoon white pepper.
- If using tomatoes, vinegar, or other acidic ingredients, add them last.
- In hard water areas, add up to 1/4 teaspoon baking soda per pound of beans to reduce cooking time.
- At high altitudes, use a pressure cooker following the manufacturer's instructions (e.g., 1/2 hour at 15 lbs pressure is equivalent to overnight cooking).
6. Mash the cooked beans in a skillet with bacon drippings, lard, oil, butter, or margarine for refried beans.
Note: Do not exceed the recommended water-to-bean ratio to prevent overflow during cooking and soaking processes.
